signal_definitions.name: AI / self-serve research pressure
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Research agencies under AI or self-serve platform pressure may need delivery leverage instead of another tool pitch.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: This can indicate willingness to consider Flashpoint as a way to defend margin, speed, or differentiation.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Research agency positioning, look for explicit AI/self-serve language, then test a delivery-leverage message.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Monitor target agencies for AI research, self-serve research, synthetic respondents, automation, and platform-disruption language.

signal_definitions.name: Survey ops or programming need
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Agencies with visible survey programming, tracking, or operational delivery needs may understand Flashpoint faster.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: This points to a concrete use case rather than a vague AI/research pitch.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Enrich account, map research ops buyer, and route to a use-case-specific discovery angle.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Watch for survey programming, research operations, tracking studies, respondent quality, and delivery-speed language.

signal_definitions.name: Bank / CPG / product innovation vertical fit
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Agencies serving banks, CPG, product, innovation, or emerging markets may map to Flashpoint proof and use cases.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: Vertical proof reduces explanation cost and makes the first wedge easier to buy.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Attach the strongest matching proof asset before copy generation.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Monitor site copy and case studies for banks, financial services, CPG, product innovation, emerging markets, and tracking.

signal_definitions.name: Tracking / monitoring recurrence
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Agencies that run trackers or recurring monitoring have a clearer path to repeat revenue than one-off research projects.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: This helps Flashpoint avoid only selling one-off projects and supports recurring/usage packaging feedback.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Prioritize for discovery if the agency also has proof-fit or ops pain.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Watch for trackers, tracking studies, brand health, concept monitoring, consumer pulse, and longitudinal research language.

signal_definitions.name: RFP differentiation pressure
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Agencies competing in RFP-heavy markets may need a sharper delivery story to win work.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: This can make Flashpoint a revenue lever for agencies, not a replacement threat.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Test message angles around RFP differentiation, speed, confidence, and delivery leverage.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Monitor for RFP, pitch, procurement, differentiation, competitive bid, and agency positioning language.

signal_definitions.name: Research ops hiring signal
signal_definitions.client_specific_insight: Hiring for research operations, survey programming, data collection, insights delivery, or client delivery can reveal active capacity gaps.
signal_definitions.why_it_matters: Hiring can show both ability to spend and willingness to fix a live operational problem.
signal_definitions.recommended_action: Run find-hiring first, then find-job-role-insights on the most relevant role before outreach.
signal_definitions.bird_dog_setup_notes: Monitor careers pages and ATS boards for research operations, survey programming, insights, data collection, project management, and client delivery roles.
